agents.backstory_hint: Expert engineer who prioritizes systemic risk and architectural integrity over surface-level code churn.
agents.goal: Determine the functional behavior change, identify risky areas, and verify test coverage in a PR.

agents.backstory_hint: Technical communicator who understands that PR reviewers need context, not just file lists.
agents.goal: Synthesize PR findings into a clear, actionable comment for reviewers.

ambiguities: How should the workflow handle PRs with merge conflicts or CI failures? Should it still comment?
assumptions: The system has programmatic access to read PR diffs and write comments.
The definition of 'trivial' (typo/comment) will be handled by the analyst agent's internal logic.

tasks.description: Examine the pull request diff to classify it as trivial or substantive. If substantive, extract behavior changes, identify risks (migrations, auth, config), and confirm test coverage.
tasks.expected_output: A technical assessment indicating if the PR is trivial or substantive, including details on behavior changes, identified risks, and test coverage status.

tasks.description: Based on the analyst's assessment, draft either a one-line summary (for trivial PRs) or a detailed summary covering changes, risks, and test coverage (for substantive PRs), then post it to the PR.
tasks.expected_output: A PR comment containing either a concise one-liner or a structured summary of the PR's impact.

tools.name: Git Diff Reader
tools.purpose: Retrieve the raw code changes from the pull request.

tools.name: PR Commenter
tools.purpose: Publish the generated analysis as a comment on the PR thread.

workflow.description: Generate a high-quality summary for pull requests to improve review quality, distinguishing between substantive changes and trivial fixes.
workflow.name: PR Summary Generator
workflow.trigger: A new pull request is opened or updated.
